title: mentha aquatica var. subsativa
intro: Mentha aquatica var. subsativa, commonly known as water mint or wild water mint, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mint family. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems. The health benefits of Mentha aquatica var. subsativa include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve headaches and nausea, and provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may help protect against chronic diseases. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or dried herb for its medicinal properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies.
